title: Imitation Of Tibullus. Sulpicia Ad Cerinthum (Lib. Quart.).
content:
Cruel Cerinthus! does the fell disease 
    Which racks my breast your fickle bosom please?
    Alas! I wish'd but to o'ercome the pain,
    That I might live for Love and you again;
    But, now, I scarcely shall bewail my fate:
    By Death alone I can avoid your hate.
meaning:
"Imitation Of Tibullus. Sulpicia Ad Cerinthum (Lib. Quart.)." is a quintessential example of George Gordon Byron's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
